title: Cannot index into a null array — RuntimeException from unguarded array subscript on null or empty collection
description: Accessing an element by numeric index (e.g. $arr[0], $parts[1]) on a variable that is $null or an empty collection throws System.Management.Automation.RuntimeException: 'Cannot index into a null array.' This differs from P022 (method/property on null) — P027 specifically targets subscript-bracket access on null/empty arrays. Common sources: (1) Get-ChildItem / Where-Object / -split / [regex]::Matches returning $null or empty, then indexing [0]; (2) .SelectedRows[0] in WinForms grids without .Count guard; (3) $Matches[1] after a failed -match; (4) string -split result indexed without element-count validation; (5) ConvertFrom-Json returning $null then indexed. Multiple fix strategies exist and must not regress each other — this pattern accommodates @() force-array, $null-ne guard, .Count-gt-0 precondition, and try/catch wrapping as valid remedies.
detection_method: Regex scan for numeric subscript access ($var[0], $var[1], $result[-1]) on variables that originate from pipeline commands, -split, regex matches, WinForms grid selections, or ConvertFrom-Json without a preceding null/count guard within 5 lines above
exception_message: System.Management.Automation.RuntimeException: Cannot index into a null array.
known_instances: Show-ScanDashboard.ps1 L403: $grid.SelectedRows[0] — WinForms grid may have no selection
Show-ScanDashboard.ps1 L591: $dgvCpsr.SelectedRows[0] — WinForms grid CPSR tab
Show-ScanDashboard.ps1 L626: $files[0].Name — Get-ScanFiles may return empty
Show-ScanDashboard.ps1 L654: $files[0].Name — report files may be empty
Invoke-ScriptDependencyMatrix.ps1 L304: $m.Groups[1].Value — regex match may fail
Invoke-ScriptDependencyMatrix.ps1 L662: $parts[1] — string -split second element may not exist
Invoke-ModuleManagement.ps1 — .Count access on pipeline results without @() guard (L211, L216, L464, L471, L519)

FIX-A-FORCE-ARRAY.label: @() force-array wrapper
FIX-A-FORCE-ARRAY.description: Wrap the source expression in @() to guarantee an array before indexing. Preferred when the source is a pipeline/cmdlet result.
FIX-A-FORCE-ARRAY.regression_risk: LOW — purely additive guard, no behaviour change when collection is non-null

FIX-B-COUNT-PRECONDITION.label: .Count -gt 0 precondition guard
FIX-B-COUNT-PRECONDITION.description: Check .Count before indexing. Suitable for arrays, lists, and DataGridView.SelectedRows.
FIX-B-COUNT-PRECONDITION.regression_risk: LOW — skips block when empty, but must ensure downstream code handles the skip

FIX-C-NULL-NE-GUARD.label: $null -ne variable guard
FIX-C-NULL-NE-GUARD.description: Explicit null check before indexing. Required when the source variable itself may be $null (not just empty).
FIX-C-NULL-NE-GUARD.regression_risk: MEDIUM — if guard is too broad it may mask logic errors; ensure else-branch is handled

FIX-D-MATCH-GUARD.label: -match success guard before $Matches[N]
FIX-D-MATCH-GUARD.description: Only access $Matches[N] inside the if-block of a successful -match. Never access $Matches after the conditional scope.
FIX-D-MATCH-GUARD.regression_risk: LOW — $Matches is only valid inside the if-true branch anyway

FIX-E-SPLIT-BOUNDS.label: -split result bounds check
FIX-E-SPLIT-BOUNDS.description: After -split, verify element count before accessing indexed elements. Prevents ArrayIndexOutOfRange on unexpected input.
FIX-E-SPLIT-BOUNDS.regression_risk: MEDIUM — if the split-count assumption is wrong, silently skips; add logging in else-branch

FIX-F-TRYCATCH-WRAP.label: try/catch fallback wrapper
FIX-F-TRYCATCH-WRAP.description: Last-resort pattern for complex expressions where inline guards are impractical. Catch RuntimeException and log.
FIX-F-TRYCATCH-WRAP.regression_risk: HIGH — masks the root cause; prefer FIX-A through FIX-E where possible

preventionRule: Never index into an array, list, or collection result without verifying it is non-null and contains sufficient elements. Preferred fix order: FIX-D (match guard) > FIX-A (@() force-array) > FIX-B (.Count precondition) > FIX-E (-split bounds) > FIX-C ($null -ne guard) > FIX-F (try/catch last resort). In WinForms handlers, always use @($grid.SelectedRows).Count -gt 0 before [0]. After -match, only use $Matches inside the if-true block. After -split, check element count before accessing indexed elements.
remedy:
# FIX-A: Force-array before index
# BEFORE (SIN)
$files = Get-ChildItem -Filter '*.json'
$first = $files[0]  # CRASH if no files
# AFTER
$files = @(Get-ChildItem -Filter '*.json')
if ($files.Count -gt 0) { $first = $files[0] }

# FIX-B: WinForms .SelectedRows guard
# BEFORE (SIN)
$row = $grid.SelectedRows[0]
# AFTER
if (@($grid.SelectedRows).Count -gt 0) {
    $row = $grid.SelectedRows[0]
}

# FIX-D: Match guard for $Matches
# BEFORE (SIN)
$line -match '(\d+)'
$num = $Matches[1]
# AFTER
if ($line -match '(\d+)') { $num = $Matches[1] }

# FIX-E: -split bounds check
# BEFORE (SIN)
$parts = $key -split '\|'
$left = $parts[0]; $right = $parts[1]
# AFTER
$parts = @($key -split '\|')
if ($parts.Count -ge 2) { $left = $parts[0]; $right = $parts[1] }
trusted_fix_notes: Pattern registered from recurring 'Cannot index into a null array' RuntimeException across Show-ScanDashboard, Invoke-ModuleManagement, and Invoke-ScriptDependencyMatrix. Six fix scenarios defined to prevent regression between competing remedies.
